fabricated_divergences.quote: Spanish-language (El País) and German-language (Tagesschau) coverage include institutional history of the facility (established 2012, 11 residential places) that is absent from UK, Pakistani, and Turkish coverage.
fabricated_divergences.why_unsupported: Only the El País analysis (article 4) mentions the facility being specialised since 2012 with 11 residential places. No Tagesschau analysis (articles 8, 11, 12) contains the 2012/11-places detail; article 12 only notes the child and mother lived at the facility. Attributing this institutional history to German-language (Tagesschau) coverage is not supported.

fabricated_divergences.quote: English-language reporting emphasizes police use of force, including details such as the number of shots fired during the car chase, whereas German-language coverage downplays these operational specifics, focusing instead on the suspect's prior police record and the formal investigative process.
fabricated_divergences.why_unsupported: German-language coverage does report police use of force: Tagesschau (article 8) explicitly describes the fleeing car being 'shot at by police,' and article 12 gives a detailed reconstruction. The corpus does not show German-language coverage systematically downplaying use-of-force specifics in favour of the investigative process; the only distinctive operational detail (15 shots) appears in the Guardian, but that does not establish the claimed group-level contrast.
